steps.title: Read the roots from the factored numerator
steps.content: Shortcut (Vieta's): The fraction equals zero only when its numerator equals zero. The numerator $(x-4)(x-9)$ is already factored, giving roots $x = 4$ and $x = 9$. The excluded value $x = 3$ is neither, so both roots are valid.

steps.title: Sum of roots directly
steps.content: Sum $= 4 + 9 = 13$. By Vieta's, the numerator expands to $x^2 - 13x + 36$, confirming sum $= -(-13)/1 = 13$. That confirms C is correct.
